question: Queen Vashti refused to come when King Ahasuerus summoned her during his banquet. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command, and the king was furious about it. Her bold refusal actually opened the door for Esther to become the next queen. God works in mysterious ways!

question: Mordecai was Esther's father. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Mordecai was Esther's cousin, not her father. When Esther's parents died, Mordecai adopted her and raised her as his own daughter. He was an amazing guardian who truly loved Esther!

question: Haman was an Amalekite, a descendant of Agag. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Haman is called 'the Agagite,' which means he was a descendant of Agag, king of the Amalekites. The Amalekites had been enemies of Israel for centuries, so this made the conflict between Haman and Mordecai even more intense!

question: Naomi and her family left Bethlehem because of a famine in the land. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! There was a famine in the land of Judah, so Elimelech took his wife Naomi and their two sons to the country of Moab to find food. It's interesting that 'Bethlehem' means 'house of bread,' but there was no bread there!

question: Ruth was originally from the land of Egypt. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Ruth was a Moabitess -- she was from the land of Moab, not Egypt. She married Naomi's son Mahlon while the family lived in Moab. Even though she was from a different nation, she chose to follow the God of Israel!

question: Boaz was a close relative of Naomi's husband Elimelech. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! The Bible says Boaz was 'a relative of Naomi's husband, a man of great wealth, of the family of Elimelech.' This family connection was very important because it made Boaz a potential kinsman-redeemer for Ruth and Naomi!

question: Hannah had many children before she prayed for Samuel. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Hannah had NO children before Samuel. The Bible says the Lord had closed her womb. That's exactly why she prayed so desperately at the tabernacle -- she longed for a child, and God answered her heartfelt prayer!

question: Eli the priest initially thought Hannah was drunk when she was praying at the tabernacle. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Because Hannah was praying silently -- her lips were moving but no sound came out -- Eli thought she had been drinking too much wine. When she explained she was pouring out her soul to the Lord, Eli blessed her and said, 'Go in peace!'

question: Hannah named her son Samuel because it means 'God has remembered.' -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Hannah named him Samuel because she said, 'I have asked for him from the Lord.' The name Samuel sounds like the Hebrew words for 'heard by God' or 'asked of God.' God heard her prayer and gave her exactly what she asked for!

question: The Bible describes Nabal as a man of good understanding and handsome. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! It was Abigail who was described as 'a woman of good understanding and beautiful appearance.' Nabal, on the other hand, was described as 'harsh and evil in his doings.' Even his name means 'fool!' What a contrast between husband and wife!

question: Abigail brought David two hundred loaves of bread as part of her provisions. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Abigail loaded up an impressive supply: two hundred loaves of bread, two skins of wine, five dressed sheep, five seahs of roasted grain, one hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs. She packed a feast for an army -- literally!

question: When Abigail told Nabal what she had done, his heart died within him and he became like a stone, and about twenty days later the Lord struck him and he died. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! The Bible says it was about TEN days later, not twenty, that the Lord struck Nabal and he died. When Abigail told him the news, his heart died within him and he became like a stone -- which sounds like he may have had a stroke or heart attack!

question: Deborah was both a prophetess and a judge of Israel. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! The Bible says Deborah was 'a prophetess, the wife of Lapidoth' who 'was judging Israel at that time.' She held two major roles at once -- she spoke God's messages AND settled disputes for the people. Talk about a multi-tasker!

question: Deborah sat under an olive tree when she judged Israel. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Deborah sat under a PALM tree, not an olive tree. It was called 'the palm tree of Deborah' and it was between Ramah and Bethel in the mountains of Ephraim. People came from all around to have her settle their disputes!

question: Barak agreed to go into battle only if Deborah would go with him. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Barak told Deborah, 'If you will go with me, then I will go; but if you will not go with me, I will not go!' Barak had great respect for Deborah's connection with God and wanted her presence and guidance on the battlefield.

question: Elizabeth was Mary's sister. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! The angel Gabriel told Mary that Elizabeth was her 'relative' (or 'cousin' in some translations), not her sister. Elizabeth was married to Zacharias the priest and was much older than Mary. They were family but not siblings!

question: When Mary greeted Elizabeth, the baby leaped in Elizabeth's womb. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby (John the Baptist) leaped in her womb and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit. Even before he was born, baby John recognized Jesus! What an amazing moment between these two mothers!

question: Zacharias was struck blind because he doubted the angel Gabriel's message. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Zacharias was struck MUTE (unable to speak), not blind. Gabriel told him, 'You will be mute and not able to speak until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words.' He couldn't talk again until John was born and named!

question: King Ahasuerus offered Esther up to half his kingdom when she came to him in the inner court. -- True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! When the king saw Esther standing in the inner court, she found favor in his sight. He held out the golden scepter and said, 'What do you wish, Queen Esther? What is your request? It shall be given to you -- up to half the kingdom!' That's a generous king!

question: Sisera was killed by Barak in the battle at Mount Tabor. -- True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Sisera was killed by Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite. After fleeing the lost battle, Sisera hid in Jael's tent. While he slept, she drove a tent peg through his temple. Deborah had prophesied that the honor of victory would go to a woman, and it did!

question: Complete Ruth's famous words: 'Entreat me not to _____ you, or to turn back from _____ you.'
explanation: Ruth's beautiful words were 'Entreat me not to leave you, or to turn back from following you.' This is one of the most famous declarations of loyalty in the whole Bible. Ruth chose to stay with Naomi no matter what -- what an incredible friend!

question: Complete the verse: 'Wherever you go, I will go; and wherever you _____, I will _____.'
explanation: Ruth told Naomi, 'Wherever you go, I will go; and wherever you lodge, I will lodge.' The NKJV uses the word 'lodge' which means to stay or spend the night. Ruth was saying she would stick with Naomi through everything!

question: Complete Ruth 1:16: 'Your _____ shall be my _____, and your God, my God.'
explanation: Ruth declared, 'Your people shall be my people, and your God, my God.' This was more than just following Naomi -- Ruth was choosing to leave behind everything she knew in Moab and fully embrace the nation and God of Israel!

question: When Boaz first noticed Ruth, he asked his servant, 'Whose _____ _____ is this?'
explanation: Boaz asked, 'Whose young woman is this?' He noticed Ruth right away in the field among the gleaners. God was already at work bringing these two together! Boaz was impressed by her from the very start.

question: Complete Mordecai's challenge to Esther: 'Yet who knows whether you have come to the _____ for such a _____ as this?'
explanation: Mordecai's famous words were 'Yet who knows whether you have come to the kingdom for such a time as this?' This powerful verse reminds us that God places us exactly where He needs us. Esther was queen for a reason -- to save her people!

question: Complete Esther's brave reply: 'And if I _____, I _____!'
explanation: Esther declared, 'And if I perish, I perish!' Before going to the king uninvited, Esther asked the Jews to fast for three days. Then she said these brave words, accepting that she might die for doing the right thing. What courage!

question: Esther asked Mordecai: 'Go, gather all the Jews who are present in _____, and _____ for me.'
explanation: Esther said, 'Go, gather all the Jews who are present in Shushan, and fast for me.' She asked for a three-day fast with no eating or drinking. Esther knew she needed the power of united prayer and fasting before facing the king!

question: Hannah prayed: 'O Lord of hosts, if You will indeed look on the _____ of Your maidservant and remember me.'
explanation: Hannah prayed asking God to look on her 'affliction.' This heartfelt prayer came from deep pain. She promised that if God gave her a son, she would give him back to the Lord for all the days of his life. And God heard her prayer!

question: Hannah's song begins: 'My _____ rejoices in the Lord; my _____ is exalted in the Lord.'
explanation: Hannah sang, 'My heart rejoices in the Lord; my horn is exalted in the Lord.' In the Bible, a 'horn' is a symbol of strength and power. Hannah was praising God because He had turned her weakness into strength by answering her prayer for a son!

question: In Hannah's song: 'The Lord kills and makes alive; He brings down to the _____ and brings _____.'
explanation: Hannah sang, 'The Lord kills and makes alive; He brings down to the grave and brings up.' Hannah's prayer showed deep theology! She understood that God has complete power over life and death. Pretty amazing wisdom for her song of praise!

question: Abigail said to David: 'Please let your maidservant speak in your _____, and hear the words of your _____.'
explanation: Abigail humbly asked David to let her 'speak in your ears' and 'hear the words of your maidservant.' She was incredibly respectful and wise in how she approached the angry warrior. Her humility and smart words saved her entire household!

question: Abigail told David: 'The life of my lord shall be bound in the _____ of the _____ with the Lord your God.'
explanation: Abigail said David's life 'shall be bound in the bundle of the living with the Lord your God.' What a beautiful way to say that God was protecting David's life like a precious treasure tied up safely in a bundle! Abigail was incredibly eloquent.

question: Deborah commanded Barak: 'Go and deploy troops at Mount _____, and take with you _____ thousand men.'
explanation: Deborah told Barak to go to Mount Tabor with ten thousand men from the tribes of Naphtali and Zebulun. She was relaying God's battle plan! God promised He would deliver Sisera and his army right into Barak's hands.

question: From the Song of Deborah: 'The stars fought from _____, the stars in their courses fought against _____.'
explanation: Deborah sang, 'The stars fought from heaven, the stars in their courses fought against Sisera.' This poetic line means that even the heavens joined the battle! God used all of creation to defeat the enemy. When God fights for you, everything is on your side!

question: Mary said: 'My _____ magnifies the Lord, and my _____ has rejoiced in God my Savior.'
explanation: Mary's famous song (called the Magnificat) begins, 'My soul magnifies the Lord, and my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior.' To 'magnify' means to make something appear greater. Mary's whole being was devoted to praising God for choosing her!
